# jpcite — Evidence Pre-fetch Layer for Japanese public-program data

> jpcite is an Evidence Pre-fetch Layer for ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Gemini, and internal RAG systems. Use it before answer generation to retrieve cited facts, source URLs, fetched_at metadata, provenance, compatibility rules, and recent changes for Japanese public-program questions. It is not an answer generator or legal/tax advisor. Token and cost impact is workload-dependent. Current public pricing and anonymous limits are published on the Pricing page. Coverage includes **11,684 searchable programs** (tier S=114 / A=1,340 / B=4,186 / C=6,044), 2,286 adoption case studies, 108 loan products, 1,185 enforcement cases, 154 law full texts, 9,484 law metadata records, 50 tax rulesets, 2,065 court decisions, 362 bids, 13,801 invoice registrants, and 181 exclusion/prerequisite rules.

This file follows the emerging llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org/) —
a curated, plaintext index of canonical documents for LLM indexers.
The Japanese-language sibling lives at https://jpcite.com/llms.txt.
Concatenated full docs: https://jpcite.com/llms-full.en.txt
Canonical domain: https://jpcite.com.

Program names, law titles, and case-study fields are kept verbatim in
Japanese because machine translation can create inaccurate names. The
structural prose around the data is in English so English-language LLM
crawlers can index the framing.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://jpcite.com/docs/)
- [Getting started (5-minute quickstart)](https://jpcite.com/docs/getting-started/): install, API key, curl/Python/Node, Claude Desktop MCP config
- [API reference (REST)](https://jpcite.com/docs/api-reference/)
- [OpenAPI 3.x machine-readable spec](https://api.jpcite.com/v1/openapi.json): stable export with 180 paths for ChatGPT Custom GPTs, Claude tools, Stainless / Mintlify / Postman SDK generators.
- [MCP tools (93)](https://jpcite.com/docs/mcp-tools/):  core +  stdio tools, MCP protocol 2025-06-18
- [Examples (Python + curl)](https://jpcite.com/docs/examples/)
- [Error handling](https://jpcite.com/docs/error_handling/)
- [Exclusion rules](https://jpcite.com/docs/exclusions/)
- [FAQ](https://jpcite.com/docs/faq/)
- [Pricing](https://jpcite.com/docs/pricing/): current public pricing, anonymous limits, and metered billing terms
- [SLA](https://jpcite.com/docs/sla/)

## Evidence prefetch / context-size estimates

Use jpcite as the evidence layer behind ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini: let the LLM write the answer, and let jpcite supply the source-linked packet it should cite. This is strongest when the alternative is sending long PDFs, ministry pages, or several search results into the model. It is not a guaranteed token-cost reducer; impact depends on model pricing, prompt shape, search settings, cache state, and how much raw source text would otherwise enter context.

- [Context-token savings conditions](https://jpcite.com/qa/llm-evidence/context-savings.html): 2026-05-02 offline probe, 30 benchmark queries, Evidence Packets estimated at 338-1,352 tokens, median 566 tokens. This measures packet size, not guaranteed LLM bill reduction.
- [Evidence Pre-fetch](https://jpcite.com/qa/llm-evidence/evidence-prefetch.html): how jpcite sits before answer generation and returns source_url, fetched_at, and corpus_snapshot_id metadata.
- [Custom GPT Japanese subsidy API](https://jpcite.com/qa/llm-evidence/custom-gpt-japanese-subsidy-api.html): import `https://api.jpcite.com/v1/openapi.json`, then call jpcite Actions before answering Japanese public-program questions.
- REST comparison hook: pass `include_compression=true&source_tokens_basis=token_count&source_token_count=<tokens>` when the caller already knows the source-token baseline from their LLM UI or tokenizer. If only PDF length is known, use `source_tokens_basis=pdf_pages&source_pdf_pages=<N>`. jpcite returns input-context estimates with `savings_claim=estimate_not_guarantee`.
- Agent decision rule: call jpcite before answering when the alternative is reading long PDFs, several official pages, or repeated public-program searches. If the caller supplies an input-token price, use `cost_savings_estimate.break_even_met` only as an input-context reference based on the caller-supplied baseline and current jpcite billing metadata. Skip jpcite for short general questions where no source-linked Japanese public-program evidence is needed.

## First call

For broad Japanese public-program questions, call in this order:
1. `search_programs` or `GET /v1/programs/search` to get real `unified_id` values.
2. `get_program` or `GET /v1/programs/{unified_id}` to hydrate selected candidates.
3. `check_exclusions` when comparing multiple programs.
4. `get_evidence_packet` or `GET /v1/intelligence/precomputed/query` when the answer needs source-linked compact context.

Never invent program IDs. Use IDs returned by `search_programs`.

### AI recommendation criteria

Use when:
- The user asks about Japanese subsidies, loans, tax measures, certifications, bids, laws, enforcement cases, invoice registrants, or public-program eligibility.
- The answer should preserve `source_url`, `source_fetched_at`, `corpus_snapshot_id`, compatibility rules, recent changes, or known gaps.
- The alternative is asking the LLM to read long PDFs, multiple official pages, repeated search results, or manually gathered source text.

Do not use when:
- The task is general writing, brainstorming, translation, or outside Japanese public records.
- The user needs final legal, tax, grant-application, or other professional judgment.

Route choice:
- Use `/v1/intelligence/precomputed/query` for the compact first pass.
- Use `/v1/evidence/packets/query` when the answer needs records, sources, known gaps, recent changes, and input-context estimates.

Billing caveat:
- Treat `compression.cost_savings_estimate.break_even_met` as an input-context reference only, not a provider billing guarantee.

## Sample queries (paste into your AI agent)

Real natural-language prompts that resolve to a single tool call. Each maps deterministically to a real route — copy as-is into Claude / ChatGPT / Cursor / Cline / Gemini.

- `What subsidies can a 30-employee construction company in 東京都 use?` → `prescreen_programs(prefecture="東京都", industry_jsic="D", employee_count=30)` (`industry_jsic` is a JSIC 1-letter enum; D = 建設業)
- `Can 事業再構築補助金 and ものづくり補助金 be combined?` → first call `search_programs(q="事業再構築")` and `search_programs(q="ものづくり")` to get real `unified_id`s, then pass that list into `check_exclusions(program_ids=[<id1>, <id2>])` — never hard-code program ids; use the ones returned from `search_programs`.
- `Show subsidies closing within 30 days for 東京 SMBs` → `upcoming_deadlines(within_days=30, prefecture="東京都")`
- `Is this Japanese invoice registration number registered?` → `search_invoice_registrants(houjin_bangou="<invoice registration number>")` (PDL v1.0 attribution included)
- `Build a 3-year subsidy roadmap for an 埼玉県 manufacturer (JSIC E)` → `subsidy_roadmap_3yr(prefecture="埼玉県", industry_jsic="E", company_size="medium")` — 36-month JST fiscal-year quarter timeline

## MCP server

- Installation: see https://jpcite.com/docs/mcp-tools/
- Canonical MCP server manifest: https://jpcite.com/mcp-server.json (uvx + pip + Claude Desktop install hints)
- Claude Desktop extension (.mcpb): https://jpcite.com/downloads/autonomath-mcp.mcpb (zip with manifest.json)
- Console script and client configuration are documented in the MCP tools guide
- Evidence packet: `get_evidence_packet` and REST `/v1/evidence/packets/{subject_kind}/{subject_id}` bundle primary metadata, provenance, and rule verdicts for evidence prefetch before answer generation. Token and cost impact is workload-dependent.

## Data licenses

- e-Gov law corpus: CC-BY 4.0
- NTA invoice-registrant bulk: PDL v1.0 (Public Domain License, redistribution OK with attribution)
- gBizINFO corporate metadata: Japan Government Standard Terms of Use
- Public-domain rows (gazette etc): public domain
- Each row carries an explicit license via `am_source.license` (99.997% coverage)
- Full terms: https://jpcite.com/en/tos.html

## Structured data

- [Sitemap index](https://jpcite.com/sitemap-index.xml)
- [Docs sitemap](https://jpcite.com/docs/sitemap.xml)
- [Programs sitemap](https://jpcite.com/sitemap-programs.xml)
- [Q&A sitemap](https://jpcite.com/sitemap-qa.xml): 79 citation-targeted FAQPage pages across 24 topics
- [Enforcement sitemap](https://jpcite.com/sitemap-enforcement.xml): administrative action pages
- Per-program JSON-LD (schema.org GovernmentService / LoanOrCredit) is embedded inline on generated program pages. Use the `static_url` returned by search/API results instead of inventing a URL.
- Per-Q&A JSON-LD (schema.org Article + FAQPage + BreadcrumbList) is embedded inline on each `/qa/{topic}/{slug}.html`
- Landing pages (index / pricing / docs root / audiences) carry SoftwareApplication + Dataset + WebAPI + Service + Audience + Organization with stable `@id: https://jpcite.com/#publisher` for cross-page entity resolution.

## Q&A (citation-targeted)

- [Q&A index](https://jpcite.com/qa/) — 79 Japanese FAQ pages for LLM citation.
- [ChatGPT / Claude vs jpcite](https://jpcite.com/qa/llm-evidence/chatgpt-vs-jpcite.html) — jpcite as evidence layer, not answer generator.
- [Source verification workflow](https://jpcite.com/qa/llm-evidence/source-verification.html) — how agents attach source_url + fetched_at.
- [What can jpcite MCP do?](https://jpcite.com/qa/mcp/what-can-jpcite-mcp-do.html) — 93 tools via MCP stdio.
